rationale: Both papers center on how economically equivalent price formats change choice, and the cited paper contributes a prospect-theory model plus experiments on partitioned trade-in framing (over/underpayment) that are conceptually close to the citing paper’s DPF vs. IPF framing mechanism work. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior price-presentation/partitioned-pricing research and refines mechanism accounts, so this citation is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention.
